variants.subject: python
variants.title: Python in 3 Hours
variants.tagline: The essential 20% that does 80% of the work

content.overview: A rapid, high-signal session covering the Python concepts that actually matter in practice. No fluff, no padding. By the end you'll be writing real scripts and know exactly where to go next.

sessions.title: The Python Mental Model
topics: The REPL and running your first script

content.prerequisites: None — just a laptop and curiosity
outcomes: Write Python scripts confidently
Understand how data flows through a program
Use third-party libraries
Know exactly what to learn next

variants.subject: python
variants.title: Python Weekend Intensive
variants.tagline: Go from zero to building projects in a single weekend

content.overview: An immersive two-day cohort that takes you from absolute basics to building three real Python projects. Small group, live instruction, immediate feedback after every block.
outcomes: Build and ship 3 complete Python projects
Understand OOP and write clean, readable code
Work with real APIs and data
Have a personalised roadmap to continue
includes: 2 weeks of doubt support via WhatsApp

variants.subject: python
variants.title: Python Mastery — 90 Days
variants.tagline: From beginner to job-ready Python developer

content.overview: A comprehensive 90-day program built for people who want to go from zero to genuinely job-ready. Weekly live sessions, graded assignments, code reviews, and a capstone project you can show employers.
outcomes: Write production-quality, tested Python code
Build and deploy REST APIs with FastAPI
Analyse data with Pandas
Ship a real capstone project for your portfolio
Be interview-ready for junior Python developer roles

variants.subject: ai
variants.title: AI Demystified in 3 Hours
variants.tagline: What AI actually is — and how it actually works

content.overview: Strip away the hype. Understand how machine learning, neural networks, and LLMs actually work — without needing a PhD. Built around visual intuition and live demos throughout.

sessions.title: What Machines Actually Learn
topics: What ML really is (vs. traditional programming)

sessions.title: Neural Networks & LLMs
topics: How a neural network learns — forward & backward pass
How GPT-4 and Claude work under the hood

sessions.title: AI in Practice
topics: Live: building with AI APIs (OpenAI / Anthropic)
Prompt engineering principles that actually work
Where AI fails — hallucinations, bias, limits
Roadmap: how to go deeper into AI

content.prerequisites: Curiosity only. No maths or coding required.
outcomes: Explain how LLMs work to anyone
Understand the real limits of current AI
Use AI APIs to build something real
Know the right learning path to go deeper

variants.subject: ai
variants.title: Build with AI — Weekend Cohort
variants.tagline: Go from AI consumer to AI builder in one weekend

content.overview: A hands-on weekend that teaches you to build real products using AI APIs — not just talk about AI. By Sunday evening you'll have shipped three working projects.

sessions.title: Project 1 & 2
topics: Project 1: AI Q&A over your own documents (RAG basics)
Project 2: AI writing assistant with memory

sessions.title: Project 3 & Deployment
topics: Project 3: your own AI micro-SaaS concept
What to build next — personal roadmap

content.prerequisites: Basic Python (variables, functions, loops). No AI knowledge needed.
outcomes: Build 3 working AI-powered applications
Understand prompt engineering deeply
Implement RAG and agent patterns
Deploy an AI product end-to-end

variants.subject: ai
variants.title: AI & ML Engineering — 12 Weeks
variants.tagline: From fundamentals to building and deploying real models

content.overview: A rigorous 12-week program for people serious about working in AI. Code-first, maths where necessary, project-driven throughout. You will train, evaluate, and deploy real models.

sessions.title: Deep Learning
topics: CNNs for vision, RNNs/LSTMs for sequences

sessions.title: Applied AI & Capstone
topics: Capstone project: your own ML product

content.prerequisites: Python basics, algebra-level maths. No prior ML experience needed.
outcomes: Train and evaluate ML models end-to-end
Build neural networks with PyTorch
Fine-tune LLMs with LoRA
Deploy models to production
Ship a real AI product for your portfolio

content.overview: A focused deep-dive into the two big ideas in calculus — derivatives and integrals — built around geometric intuition first and formulas second. You'll leave actually understanding why the rules work.

sessions.title: Derivatives
topics: What a derivative really means — the slope idea
Chain rule, product rule — explained visually

sessions.title: Applications & ML Connections
topics: Where calculus appears in ML (gradient descent)

content.prerequisites: Basic algebra and familiarity with functions (y = f(x))
outcomes: Differentiate any standard function confidently
Evaluate definite and indefinite integrals
Apply calculus to real optimisation problems
Understand why gradient descent works

variants.subject: maths
variants.title: Linear Algebra for ML in 3 Hours
variants.tagline: The maths behind neural networks — made visual

content.overview: Every AI model is built on linear algebra. This session builds a visual, geometric intuition for vectors, matrices, and transformations — exactly the parts that show up in ML.

sessions.title: Vectors & Spaces
topics: Vector operations — addition, scaling, dot product

sessions.title: Matrices as Transformations
topics: Matrix multiplication as composition of transformations

sessions.title: Eigenvalues, PCA & Neural Nets
topics: How weights in neural networks are matrices

content.prerequisites: Basic algebra. Calculus helpful but not required.
outcomes: Understand matrix operations geometrically
Explain PCA and embeddings intuitively
Read ML papers without freezing at the maths
Use NumPy confidently for linear algebra

variants.subject: maths
variants.title: Maths for ML — Power Weekend
variants.tagline: Calculus + Linear Algebra + Probability in one focused weekend

content.overview: The complete maths toolkit for machine learning — taught in two intense, focused days. Designed for people who want to understand AI deeply, not just use it.

sessions.title: Tying it Together
topics: How all three areas combine in a neural network
Reading an ML paper's maths section

content.prerequisites: 12th grade maths (algebra, basic trigonometry). No calculus experience needed.
outcomes: Understand gradient descent mathematically
Read ML papers without fear
Use NumPy for matrix operations
Solve probability problems for ML interviews

variants.subject: technology
variants.title: Build Your First Website — 3 Weeks
variants.tagline: HTML, CSS, JavaScript — from blank page to deployed site

content.overview: Three focused weeks to go from nothing to a live, deployed personal portfolio website. You build something real every session — no toy exercises.

sessions.title: JavaScript
topics: DOM manipulation — changing the page live
Fetch API — pulling data from the internet

outcomes: Build a responsive, deployed personal website
Understand HTML, CSS and JavaScript fundamentals
Use Git and deploy to Vercel
Know exactly what to learn next (React, etc.)

variants.subject: technology
variants.title: React Foundations — 4 Weeks
variants.tagline: Build modern UIs with the world's most popular frontend library

content.overview: A focused four-week sprint through React — the library that powers most modern web apps. You'll build three real projects and understand the mental model deeply.

sessions.title: React Mental Model
topics: Why React — components, JSX, the virtual DOM

content.prerequisites: HTML, CSS, basic JavaScript (variables, functions, events)
outcomes: Build multi-page React applications
Manage state and side effects correctly
Create custom hooks
Deploy React apps to production

variants.subject: science
variants.title: Classical Mechanics Weekend
variants.tagline: Newton to energy conservation — one focused weekend

content.overview: A rigorous but accessible weekend covering the core of classical mechanics. Physical intuition first — equations follow naturally. By Day 2 you'll be solving problems you never thought you could.
content.prerequisites: Basic algebra. 11th grade physics helpful but not required.
outcomes: Solve mechanics problems from first principles
Build genuine physical intuition
Prepared for university physics or JEE/NEET mechanics

variants.subject: programming
variants.title: DSA for Interviews — 6 Weeks
variants.tagline: Everything you need to crack coding interviews at product companies

content.overview: A focused 6-week course covering every data structure and algorithm pattern that appears in technical interviews. Pattern-first teaching — you learn to recognise the problem type, not just memorise solutions.

sessions.title: Recursion & Dynamic Programming
topics: Classic DP: knapsack, LCS, coin change

sessions.title: Advanced Topics & Mock Interviews
topics: 3 full mock interviews with detailed written feedback

content.prerequisites: Python (or any language) basics — variables, loops, functions, classes
outcomes: Solve medium LeetCode problems consistently
Recognise and apply 15+ algorithm patterns
Perform confidently under interview conditions
Be ready for technical screens at product companies

variants.subject: python
variants.title: Python for Data Science — 8 Weeks
variants.tagline: NumPy, Pandas, Matplotlib and the full data workflow

content.overview: A structured 8-week program covering the complete Python data science stack — from raw data to actionable insights. Project-driven: every week ends with a real dataset.

sessions.title: Pandas — Data Wrangling
topics: Project: clean a messy real-world dataset

sessions.title: Visualisation & EDA
topics: Project: full EDA on Indian census data

content.prerequisites: Basic Python (variables, loops, functions). No statistics background needed.
outcomes: Clean, transform and analyse real datasets with Pandas
Create clear, publication-quality visualisations
Run an end-to-end EDA workflow
Present findings clearly in Jupyter notebooks
